violations.description: Dynamic provider event IDs are generated with Date.now(), which can make failures harder to replay exactly.
violations.suggestion: Route event-id generation through a deterministic test helper with seeded or injected clock values.

violations.description: UI automation path also uses Date.now() derived IDs, introducing non-repeatable payload values.
violations.suggestion: Use shared deterministic ID factory for webhook payloads in both API and E2E specs.

recommendations: Centralize deterministic provider-event ID generation in test factories.
Inject a test clock or deterministic suffix generator where event IDs are required.
Keep sort logic deterministic by asserting canonical order against stable fixtures.
summary: Determinism is strong overall; minor non-determinism comes from Date.now()-derived IDs in a few specs.
